title: [DefaultValue] should be avoided when [DefaultParameterValue] is meant
description: DefaultValue does not make the compiler set the default value, as its name may suggest. What you probably wanted to use is DefaultParameterValue. The DefaultValue attribute from the System.ComponentModel namespace, is sometimes used to declare a member’s default value.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

logic.reasoning: [DefaultValue] from System.ComponentModel does NOT set parameter defaults at compile time - it's for metadata only. Developers should use [DefaultParameterValue] from System.Runtime.InteropServices OR C# default parameter syntax (param = value). Detects [DefaultValue] in method parameter lists.

should_not_trigger: public void DoStuff([Optional, DefaultParameterValue(4)] int i)

checks.message: Use [DefaultParameterValue] or C# default parameters instead of [DefaultValue] on method parameters
